description: Overview of the fundamental concepts of training methods: principles for developing endurance, strength, speed, flexibility, and coordination.

questions.explanation: A training method is a systematic procedure applied to achieve a training goal, defining how the load is organised and administered.

options.text: The name of the training facility
A systematic procedure applied to achieve a training goal
The rules of the competition system

questions.text: Continuous running is one of the basic methods for developing endurance.
questions.explanation: True. Sustained running at a steady pace (the continuous method) is a fundamental tool for developing aerobic endurance.

questions.text: What is the essence of interval training?
questions.explanation: Interval training alternates intense work phases with partial recovery phases, thereby developing both aerobic and anaerobic capacity simultaneously.

options.text: Alternating intense work phases with partial recovery phases
Maximal effort sustained over a long duration

questions.text: The basic method for strength development is training with progressively increasing resistance.
questions.explanation: True. Progressive overload (e.g., increasing weights) continuously triggers muscular adaptation, thereby developing strength.

questions.text: Which method best develops reaction speed?
questions.explanation: Reaction speed is best developed through short, maximal-intensity sprints initiated on a signal and sport-specific tasks that require rapid decision-making.

options.text: Short, maximal-intensity tasks initiated on a signal

questions.text: What type of stretching most effectively develops flexibility?
questions.explanation: The most effective method for developing flexibility is static stretching: holding a position for 20–30 seconds increases joint range of motion.

questions.text: Complex and varied movement tasks are suitable for developing coordination.
questions.explanation: True. Coordination can be effectively developed through complex tasks (ball exercises, ladder drills, balance tasks) because these require the coordinated work of multiple muscle groups.

questions.text: Why is choosing the appropriate training method important?
questions.explanation: Choosing the appropriate method ensures that the load precisely targets the desired ability — with the wrong method, the desired development cannot be achieved.

options.text: So that the training session is as long as possible
So that the load precisely targets the desired ability
So that the athlete does not get tired
So that the competition ends sooner

questions.text: What is the essence of circuit training?
questions.explanation: In circuit training, different exercise stations are performed in sequence with short rest periods, simultaneously developing strength, endurance, and coordination.

options.text: Repeated execution of a single exercise
Different exercise stations performed in sequence with short rest periods
A series of static stretching positions

questions.text: Every training method is suitable for developing every ability.
questions.explanation: False. Each method primarily affects specific abilities (e.g., interval training → aerobic/anaerobic; static stretching → flexibility). Correct method selection is the foundation of training design.
